etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic 荅(荅) (dá)
etymology.interpretation: The character 塔(tǎ, "tower; pagoda") combines the semantic component 土(土) (tǔ, "earth"), referring to a structure built on the ground, with the phonetic component 荅(荅) (dá), which provides the sound. The concept of a 'tower or pagoda' is solidified by the idea of an earth-bound, constructed structure.

examples.chinese: 那座塔很高(那座塔很高)。
他们正在修建一座新的佛塔(他們正在修建一座新的佛塔)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhèngzài xiūjiàn yī zuò xīn de fótǎ.
examples.english: They are constructing a new pagoda.

examples.chinese: 巴黎铁塔是世界闻名的地标(巴黎鐵塔是世界聞名的地標)。
examples.pinyin: Bālí tiětǎ shì shìjiè wénmíng de dìbiāo.
examples.english: The Eiffel Tower is a world-famous landmark.
